risks: Standard_LRS storage provides no zone or geo redundancy; single-zone failure could cause data loss
No Key Vault resource for secret and credential management across services
No private endpoints or network isolation configured for Databricks, Synapse, or Storage
No monitoring, diagnostics, or alerting resources (e.g., Log Analytics, Azure Monitor)
No explicit managed identity configuration for inter-service authentication (ADF→Storage, Databricks→Storage, Synapse→Storage)
Storage account local auth (key access) not explicitly disabled per Azure security best practices
No resource tagging strategy defined for cost allocation and operational governance
correctionsRecommended: Upgrade storage SKU from Standard_LRS to Standard_ZRS or Standard_GRS for production redundancy
Add an Azure Key Vault resource for centralized secret and credential management
Configure system-assigned managed identities for ADF, Databricks, and Synapse with RBAC roles on the storage account
Add private endpoints for Storage, Synapse, and Databricks to restrict public network access
Add a Log Analytics workspace and Azure Monitor resource for diagnostics, alerting, and pipeline monitoring
Explicitly disable storage account local auth (shared key access) and public blob access
Define a consistent resource tagging strategy (e.g., environment, cost-center, owner)
Consider VNet integration for the Databricks workspace to enable secure cluster connectivity
